steps.title: Use the guild expertise
steps.content: The craft guilds are described as "highly discerning producers and consumers of material cultures." That means members could judge handmade objects and fixtures with expert attention, so gifting such objects could display skill to knowledgeable peers.

steps.title: Reject unsupported effects
steps.content: A invents secrecy about materials and techniques. B creates an unsupported contrast between large guilds and craft guilds. D claims gifts carried greater status when sent to large guilds, but the passage focuses on gifts within craft guilds. The remaining inference fits the peer-reputation context. The answer is C.
